title: HiWhyV Aims to Build Swedenâ€™s First Large-Scale Hydrogen Valley
content: HiWhyV aims to establish Swedenâ€™s first large-scale hydrogen valley by linking the West Coast, a major center of industrial hydrogen demand, with the High Coast, one of Europeâ€™s most favourable regions for renewable hydrogen production. The project could produce up to 123,000 tonnes of renewable hydrogen annually, supporting applications such as eâ€‘fuels, green fertilisers, sponge iron, and hydrogen-based transport. It will also reuse residual oxygen and waste heat in aquaculture and district heating to enhance efficiency and sustainability. Supported by the Clean Hydrogen Partnership, HiWhyV will demonstrate integrated hydrogen value chains at commercial scale and pioneer a demandâ€‘led deployment model. Replication actions in Germany, Spain, and Greece aim to strengthen Swedenâ€™s role in Europeâ€™s hydrogen economy and provide a blueprint for future hydrogen valleys across the EU
author: Analysts Account
